description:
You are a junior data-analyst who has been handed three quarterly sales CSV files that live in the
    directory
    
        /home/user/data/
    
    The files are named exactly
    
        Q1_sales.csv
        Q2_sales.csv
        Q3_sales.csv
    
    Each of the three files already contains a header row followed by the raw sales data.  
    Management wants a single, clean data set that contains only the orders which match BOTH of the
    following rules:

        1. The Status column must be the literal word “Completed”.
        2. The Amount column must be an integer that is greater than, or equal to, 1000.

    Your assignment is to:

    1. Create a new directory called
    
           /home/user/output/
    
       (if it does not already exist).
    
    2. Produce one consolidated CSV file named
    
           /home/user/output/filtered_sales.csv
    
       The file MUST satisfy all of these requirements so that the automated grader can verify it:

           • The very first line is the original header line
             “OrderID,Product,Amount,Status”.
           • The remaining lines appear in the following fixed order:
               – all matching lines from Q1_sales.csv (in the same order they
                 appeared in Q1_sales.csv),
               – followed by all matching lines from Q2_sales.csv,
               – followed by all matching lines from Q3_sales.csv).
           • Fields are comma-separated with NO surrounding spaces.
           • There are NO extra blank lines at the end of the file.

       In other words, the file must look exactly like this:

           OrderID,Product,Amount,Status
           1001,Laptop,1200,Completed
           1006,Server,2500,Completed
           2005,Camera,1100,Completed
           3002,Switch,1200,Completed
           3004,Laptop,1400,Completed

    3. For auditing purposes, create a plain-text processing log called
    
           /home/user/output/process.log
    
       The log must contain precisely three lines—one for each source file—using this rigid format:

           YYYY-MM-DD <filename> <records_before> <records_after>

       where:
           • YYYY-MM-DD is the fixed literal date 2024-01-01 (do NOT use the
             current date or a time stamp),
           • <filename> is the exact source CSV file name,
           • <records_before> is the total number of data rows (excluding the
             header) that were read from the file,
           • <records_after> is the number of rows that met the filter
             criteria and were written into filtered_sales.csv.

       When you are finished, process.log must read exactly (including the single spaces):

           2024-01-01 Q1_sales.csv 6 2
           2024-01-01 Q2_sales.csv 5 1
           2024-01-01 Q3_sales.csv 5 2

    4. Use any combination of standard GNU/Linux command-line tools
       (grep, awk, sed, cut, sort, cat, etc.).  The whole job can be done in
       roughly 5–10 commands.

    5. Your script or ad-hoc command sequence should run successfully from a
       fresh shell on a typical Debian/Ubuntu environment where the only
       pre-existing files are those mentioned above.

    Reminder: the grader will look ONLY at
        /home/user/output/filtered_sales.csv
        /home/user/output/process.log
    and will compare them byte-for-byte with the reference answers, so follow
    the required formats exactly.

        3001,Router,90,Completed
        3002,Switch,1200,Completed
        3003,Desk,320,Cancelled
        3004,Laptop,1400,Completed

        Expected final state created by the agent:

        /home/user/output/filtered_sales.csv
        --------------------------------
        OrderID,Product,Amount,Status
        1001,Laptop,1200,Completed
        1006,Server,2500,Completed
        2005,Camera,1100,Completed
        3002,Switch,1200,Completed
        3004,Laptop,1400,Completed

        /home/user/output/process.log
        --------------------------------
        2024-01-01 Q1_sales.csv 6 2
        2024-01-01 Q2_sales.csv 5 1
        2024-01-01 Q3_sales.csv 5 2
    -->
